scan.zone_map_skip: skip block b if zm[b].min_uom > usd_code || zm[b].max_uom < usd_code

filters.notes: sub_fy flat array (172KB) resident in L1; O(1) lookup

join_order.notes: adsh_code == sub row index; sub_fy[adsh_code] == 2022 is O(1)

data_structures.sub_fy_array: flat_array int16_t[86135] — loaded once, used in scan loop
data_structures.adsh_group_id: flat_array int32_t[86135] — compact group index (0..n_groups-1) or -1; built during data_loading by grouping fy=2022 sub rows by (name_string, cik)
data_structures.adsh_cik_idx: flat_array int32_t[86135] — compact cik index (0..n_ciks-1) or -1; built during data_loading alongside adsh_group_id using a cik→idx hash map over fy=2022 rows
data_structures.local_group_sum: flat_array long double[n_groups] per thread — indexed directly by adsh_group_id[adsh_code]; no hash probing; initialized to 0.0
data_structures.local_cik_sum: flat_array double[n_ciks] per thread — indexed directly by adsh_cik_idx[adsh_code]; no hash probing; initialized to 0.0
data_structures.merged_group_sum: flat_array long double[n_groups] — element-wise reduction of per-thread local_group_sum after scan
data_structures.merged_cik_sum: flat_array double[n_ciks] — element-wise reduction of per-thread local_cik_sum after scan

parallelism.notes: Single fused OMP parallel for over active_blocks (schedule dynamic,8). Each thread owns local_group_sum[long double] and local_cik_sum[double] flat arrays. After scan loop, reduce by iterating local arrays in a second parallel or serial reduction step. Flat array sizes: n_groups ~25000 (400KB long double), n_ciks ~17000 (136KB double). Total thread-local footprint: ~536KB/thread × 64 threads = ~34MB < 44MB L3.

fused_scan_logic.description: Single parallel pass computing both cik_sum (for HAVING threshold) and group_sum (for output) from identical filter. Eliminates second full-table scan.
fused_scan_logic.inner_loop: for each row r in block: if uom[r]==usd_code && fy_arr[adsh[r]]==2022: local_group_sum[grp_arr[adsh[r]]] += val[r]; local_cik_sum[cik_idx_arr[adsh[r]]] += val[r]
fused_scan_logic.post_scan: merge local_group_sum arrays element-wise (OMP parallel for over n_groups); merge local_cik_sum arrays element-wise (serial or OMP over n_ciks); avg_threshold = sum(merged_cik_sum) / n_ciks

having.threshold_source: sum(merged_cik_sum) / n_ciks (computed after single fused scan)

output.decode_step: for each gid where merged_group_sum[gid] > avg_threshold: name via name_offsets/name_data[groups[gid].rep_adsh], cik = groups[gid].cik

optimization_notes: Root cause: two problems in the logical plan. First, pass 1 (cik aggregation for HAVING threshold) was single-threaded despite 64 available cores, consuming 205ms. The sequential constraint was unnecessary because avg_threshold is used only in a > comparison where floating-point accumulation order at the margin is inconsequential. Second, two separate full-table scans (298ms total) over identical data with identical filters, when both accumulators can be computed in a single fused pass. Strategy: Category A (plan restructuring) — fuse pass 1 and pass 2 into one single parallel scan that simultaneously accumulates local_group_sum (for output) and local_cik_sum (for threshold), both as flat arrays indexed by pre-built compact integer keys. Category B (algorithm change) — replace CompactHashMap probing with direct flat array indexing: adsh_group_id[ac] (0..n_groups-1) already is a compact index; build adsh_cik_idx[ac] (0..n_ciks-1) during data_loading. Thread-local flat arrays (400KB long double for groups + 136KB double for ciks = 536KB/thread × 64 = 34MB) fit within the 44MB L3, giving cache-resident random-access updates with zero hash overhead. Post-scan element-wise merge of small flat arrays is trivially fast. Expected outcome: single parallel scan replaces two (one sequential + one parallel), with direct array accumulators eliminating hash probing entirely.
